articles.title: FIVERR WANTS GIG WORKERS TO OFFLOAD SOME OF THEIR WORK TO AI
articles.summary: Fiverr has introduced AI tools that let freelancers train models on their work and monetize AI-generated content. Its Personal AI Creation Model costs $25 monthly, allowing gig workers to retain ownership of AI-generated assets. Fiverr also launched an AI assistant and a stock-sharing program for top-performing freelancers.

articles.title: NIKE TEAMS UP WITH SKIMS ON A NEW WOMEN'S BRAND
articles.summary: NikeSkims merges Nike's innovation and athlete insights with Skims' style and inclusivity to launch a new women's brand this spring. The collection includes apparel, footwear, and accessories, expanding Skims into sportswear while strengthening Nike's athleisure presence. This partnership empowers women and marks a shift beyond traditional performance wear.

articles.title: SUPERHUMAN INTRODUCES AI-POWERED CATEGORIZATION TO REDUCE SPAMMY EMAILS IN YOUR INBOX
articles.summary: Superhuman's new AI-powered Auto Label feature categorizes emails into predefined or custom labels, such as marketing, pitch, and social. It also enhances reminders with AI-driven follow-ups, surfacing unanswered emails, and drafting responses in the user's tone. Superhuman plans to integrate personal knowledge bases and automate email workflows for smarter inbox management.

articles.title: “BEWARE THE BLUE APPLE” – UNIVERSAL DESIGN VS CULTURAL NUANCE
articles.summary: Language and culture shape perception and influence design choices, affecting how users interact with products and services. Designers must consider cultural nuances, language differences, and localization to create effective, user-friendly designs.

articles.title: HOW DO WE KNOW WHAT COMPONENTS TO WORK ON?
articles.summary: Prioritizing design system components requires an impact vs effort matrix, identifying missing elements, ensuring completeness, tracking usage, and running prioritization planning. Confidence in estimations improves decision-making. Adoption tracking helps assess component usage. Prioritization balances new, completed, and improved components based on team goals and maturity level, ensuring an effective design system.

articles.title: THE ROLE OF CLARITY IN CREATIVE PROJECT BRIEFS
articles.summary: A well-defined project brief ensures clarity and aligns teams toward shared goals. It prevents miscommunication, enhances collaboration, and streamlines workflows. Key elements include clear objectives, audience insights, timelines, and feedback loops. Emphasizing design constraints, expectations, and visual communication fosters creativity within practical limits, resulting in more effective and impactful design outcomes.

articles.title: BUILD STUNNING FORM PAGES IN MINUTES
articles.summary: Customize your form and easily share it with the world. Perfect for collecting leads, signups, or feedback.

articles.title: PATTERN CLUB
articles.summary: A curated collection of patterns, textures, and visuals from designers and illustrators.

articles.title: PALETTE LIST
articles.summary: The Lospec Palette List is a database of palettes for pixel art, with palettes for old hardware that can only display a few colors and others created by pixel artists specifically for making art.

articles.title: INCLUSIVE SANS MAKES THE CASE FOR ACCESSIBILITY BEING STANDARD, NOT SECONDARY
articles.summary: Inclusive Sans, designed by Olivia King, is a highly readable, open-source typeface focused on accessibility. Available on Google Fonts, it offers a 10-style family with five weights and matching italics for diverse applications.

articles.title: TELGEA'S NEW BRANDING: CONNECTING CONTINENTS WITH A UNIFIED VISUAL IDENTITY
articles.summary: Telgea's new branding, designed by Signifly, features a minimalist sphere logo called "The Dot" to symbolize global connectivity. This refreshed identity balances professionalism with creativity, using vibrant visuals and intuitive data representation.

articles.title: REIMAGINING FLUID TYPOGRAPHY
articles.summary: Fluid typography should respect user preferences without relying on fixed assumptions. Using em and rem maintains internal harmony, but viewport-relative units introduce scaling issues. Browser defaults often differ, making pixel-based conversions unreliable. The best approach is to avoid setting a root font size, allowing user-defined preferences to control text scaling naturally.

articles.title: ILLUSTRATOR POAN PAN ON EMBRACING CHANGE, COMMUNITY, AND THE UNEXPECTED
articles.summary: Poan Pan has evolved from a freelance illustrator into a multidisciplinary artist that embraces creative exploration, new mediums, and collaborations while challenging industry norms.

articles.title: THE DARK SIDE OF UX: LESSONS FROM ONLYFANS' ADDICTIVE DESIGN
articles.summary: OnlyFans' UX blends accessibility and exclusivity to foster addictive engagement.

articles.title: THE SUPERB GAME CONCEPT ARTWORKS BY YINZHENG
articles.summary: Yinzheng, a Chinese illustrator created the concept art for the game Naraka: Bladepoint, showcasing impressive artistic skills in game design.
